title: Melinis longiseta
taxonomy: Melinis longiseta is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. The genus Melinis is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae. Melinis longiseta is native to tropical regions of Africa and Madagascar. This species is characterized by its long, narrow leaves and distinctive seed head. It is often used as an ornamental plant or for erosion control.

intro: Melinis longiseta, also known as red grass or bush grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Melinis longiseta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Melinis longiseta is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
